summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments in small UAS operations. Ukraine's drone operators have wiped out a Russian FPV drone crew on the Huliaipole front and destroyed a Russian Ka-27 helicopter using an FPV drone launched from a naval drone, marking the first such strike in military history. Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground "mother drone" for carrying FPVs, and the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone, while also introducing the Ptashka, which smashes strike quadcopter records by flying on a glass thread invisible to Russian forces. The T-80BVM tank has survived 18 Ukrainian FPV drone strikes, and Russia is deploying knockoffs of Ukraine's Vampire heavy bomber drones, with the 81st Brigade already shooting down five. Ukraine's I-SEE system can now track Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals, and the country's Shrike Fiber drone has won top place in a Pentagon drone procurement program.
